Bitmine Immersion Technologies is an Ethereum treasury vehicle holding 5.42 million ETH marked at $10.86 billion (May 31, 2026) against 579.7 million diluted shares after aggressive ATM issuance. Legacy immersion mining is immaterial; staking revenue is growing but nine-month general and administrative expense was $335.7 million. The stock trades below May filing ETH fair value per share, offering partial token-stack cushion but not protection from unbounded dilution. Stance watch (below mid-teens accumulate hurdle).

Stance watch until binding MWI equity and an in-service date.

tickers.one_line_thesis: CMSG is a cash-rich OTCQX bitcoin/scrypt miner where marked-to-market digital assets dominate GAAP earnings; the equity is largely a BTC-treasury and hash-share option under Horizon-affiliated governance — watch at 8.7% blended IRR (Marvin floor 7.7%); approved Substacks (LCI seigniorage) add partial treasury credit; fee redesign post-2026 and network-share trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary: CMSG is an optionality name: loss-making mining ops plus a cash-rich treasury and Horizon-related governance. Normalized owner earnings (~$1.35/sh) blend interest income and breakeven mining, excluding crypto marks. At ~$30 (OTCQX, ~May 26, 2026), base expected annual return at today's price (Lawrence) is 8.81%, below the 15% bar; bull 18.8% requires BTC/treasury re-rating. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is unproven; asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is partial via NAV floor. We propose watch and size for OTC liquidity; reconcile with any FRMO/Horizon sleeve overlap.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global salvage-vehicle auction toll booth de-rated to ~$33 — oligopoly economics intact; base IRR 9.2% (core override) while monitoring U.S. assignment/fee growth and Q3 FY2026 trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Copart is a global salvage-auction oligopoly with fortress balance sheet, yard network scale, and float economics. Q3 FY2026 showed $1.24B revenue (+2.1%) with international strength and ASP +4.6% offsetting US unit decline. At ~$33.07, Lawrence synthesis implies 10.78% per year over 7 years on $1.25 mid-cycle free cash flow per share. Comp-based land work lifts economic book from ~$9.40 (filed GAAP) to ~$14.02 per share; land alone moves from ~$2.45 to ~$7.07 per share at base marks. Primary stance gate remains operating cash flow, not book. Dhando is full. We propose watch on IRR math.

Base case annual return is 11.3% per year on a $7 per share risked payoff in about four years. That is below our usual fift...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Gyrodyne, LLC is winding itself down and selling its last two real estate properties (Flowerfield on Long Island and Cortlandt Manor in Westchester County). The company's own disclosed liquidation-basis net asset estimate is $11.79 per share as of March 31, 2026, versus a market price near $5.80, a roughly 51% discount. If that estimate is realized by the company's own stated 2028 target dissolution date, the annualized return is about 33% per year; even a stressed bear case (extra year of delay, weaker price on the one signed land-sale contract) still returns a positive 13% to 14% per year.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Gyrodyne is winding itself down. It owns two properties on Long Island and in Westchester County, is pursuing zoning approvals to raise their sale value, and intends to dissolve once everything is sold, likely by the end of 2028. The company discloses its own estimate of what shareholders should receive: about $11.79 per share as of March 31, 2026, versus a market price near $5.80. If that estimate holds and the timeline holds, buying today and collecting that amount works out to about 33.4% per year. We treat this as an asset story, not an earnings story: the return depends on approvals, a...

deep_dive.executive_summary: HEE (EXAE.AT) is Greece's national exchange croupier: trading, clearing, listing, data, and digital services. FY2025 filings show €86.3 million turnover, €42.2 million EBITDA, €31.6 million net income, and €53.6 million operating cash flow on 57.85 million shares. Euronext holds 74.25% after its November 2025 tender. At €7.49 per share (Yahoo EXAE.AT, 2026-06-11), we expect 10.86% per year over 7 years on €0.42 mid-cycle owner cash per share, below our 15% core hurdle. Dhando is partial: monopoly franchise and net cash, but cyclical fees and Euronext control limit upside. We propose watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Intercontinental Exchange is a toll collector on global market activity: exchanges, clearing, data, and mortgage rails with minimal principal risk. FY2025 filings confirm $9,931M net revenues, $4,929M operating income, and $28,915M stockholders' equity; Q1 2026 accelerated to $2,977M net revenues and $1,413M net income on strong Exchanges volume. At $153 per share, we expect about 14.18% per year over 7 years on $7.41 mid-cycle free cash flow per share (below our about-fifteen-percent-per-year target for a core hold). Near-term earnings per share would look higher if Q1 2026 trading volumes...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Michigan mineral royalty optionality (>1M acres, Copperwood/Highland Copper leases) with patient balance sheet — asymmetric upside if mining develops, steady burn and single-tenant concentration until royalties activate.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Keweenaw Land is a Michigan mineral estate with a Copperwood free option on top of modest lease income. We expect -8.29% per year (total synthesis at today's price). FY2024 GAAP book is ~$12.70 per share; economic floor about $21 per share, not GAAP book. Spot copper ~$6.67/lb (2026-06-02) scales the SSI royalty bridge to ~$12.8M/yr; option yield about 20.7% at this price. Management expects cash-flow positive from 2026 at the 2025 annual shareholder meeting [Management statement] (unverified vs H1 2025 net loss). Watch: price still embeds much of the option despite improved spot Cu.

FY2025 AFFO of $0.393 per share covered only ~70% of the $0.56 annual dividend; Q1 2026 coverage fell to ~54% as fixed base rent declined after dispositions while participation rent remained lumpy. Leverage is manageable (~$486 million total indebtedness, mostly fixed-rate) but Q1 ATM issuance added 3.7 million shares. At ~$9.23, our base case annual return is 2.2% per year on normalized owner cash of $0.38 per share...

deep_dive.executive_summary: LandBridge is a fee-based Delaware Basin land manager with strong operating proof: FY2025 revenue $199M (+81%), adjusted EBITDA $177M, and $120M consolidated free cash flow. Q1 2026 revenue $51M (+16% YoY) with free cash flow $41M and raised 2026 adjusted EBITDA outlook $210–230M. The asset base (315k+ acres) and WaterBridge ecosystem tie are strategically valuable, but Class A investors receive only ~42% of consolidated net income and face ~$560M long-term debt plus sponsor governance. At ~$73 (NYSE, June 2, 2026), we expect 1.83% per year on $1.82 starting free cash flow per Class A share...

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Current surface, resource, and royalty fee engine
components.assumption_summary: Enterprise value before net debt; the base multiple is deliberately generous for scarce, capital-light acreage.
components.cross_check: Q1 2026 revenue $51.0M, EBITDA $44.9M, FCF $40.9M; do not separately add currently monetized acreage.

components.label: Net debt and financing claims
components.cross_check: Base approximates $505M net debt divided by 77.017M economic units.

components.label: Dormant and under-monetized acreage inventory
components.cross_check: The remaining acreage and all current revenue are embedded in the fee-engine valuation.

components.label: Alpha Digital 2 GW campus option
components.cross_check: Do not promote the high sensitivity to base until an executed agreement establishes LB's net claim after capital and the relevant milestone conditions. Samsung options are capacity options, not operating rent.

components.label: Pore space and other new-use options
components.cross_check: Options graduate into current operations only after contracts establish economics.

economic_value.accounting_reference: The 2025 10-K allocated recent purchases among land, surface-use agreements, and brackish-water assets; these allocations are cross-checks, not the valuation conclusion.

economic_claim.description: Each listed Class A share represents one OpCo unit. Valuation therefore uses 27.839 million Class A plus 49.178 million exchangeable Class B/OpCo units, rather than dividing consolidated cash flow by Class A shares alone. Sponsor control, tax distributions, and related-party WaterBridge transactions remain governance risks.
economic_claim.unit_source: valuation.inputs.shares_outstanding
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: The complete additive component schedule includes operating assets, financial assets, debt or financing claims, minority interests where applicable, and realization reserves before division by the stated economic units.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Current surface, resource, and royalty fee engine
valuation_proof.evidence: 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance is $210M-$230M; values use $180M at 10x, $220M at 15x, and $240M at 20x.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Currently monetized acreage, water royalties, and surface-use contracts are embedded here and not added as full land NAV.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Utilization, incremental after-tax return on capital, or contract economics fall below the low-case assumptions.

valuation_proof.evidence: Q1 2026 long-term debt was approximately $535M; year-end cash was $30.7M.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Deducted once from enterprise value.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The obligation, financing claim, tax, or realization friction is materially larger than the low-case reserve.

valuation_proof.evidence: LandBridge purchases ranged from approximately $875 per acre for an 800-acre bolt-on to roughly $7,100 per fee acre in the 1918 Ranch allocation. Only an illustrative 20% of 320k acres is separately marked after overlap and realization discounts.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The remaining acreage is embedded in the fee engine.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Discounting, realization friction, and the component assumptions reflect the time required to contract, develop, or monetize the claim.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The component low/base/high range or milestone driver explicitly risks realization; uncertainty widens the range rather than creating a zero.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining owner-funded capital is deducted in the driver where disclosed; otherwise the range and realization reserve reflect the unresolved capital burden.

valuation_proof.evidence: The local primary packet supports only prospective digital-infrastructure use and a PowerBridge option over up to 3,400 acres, with first power targeted for 2027 and scaled generation for 2028. It does not contain an executed Alpha Digital lease or development agreement stating customer, rent or participation, capital obligations, phasing, commencement conditions, or termination rights. Base value is therefore zero; $12 per share is an upside sensitivity, not contracted rent.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: No full campus value is capitalized before customer, power, and lease economics are contractual.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Mesabi Trust is a contractual pellet royalty on Cliffs' Northshore mine with cyclical commodity exposure and a legal recovery option. FY2026 10-K confirms AAA arbitration commenced September 26, 2025 on idling and intercompany pricing; Horizon Kinetics holds 22.9%. Q1 2026 bonus royalty was zero despite tonnage doubling YoY; Q4 2025 still paid $1.04 million bonus, showing mechanics intact. Nine-month royalty income fell to $13.16 million on lower pricing and a February 2025 maintenance shutdown. At $25.68 (Stooq, May 26, 2026), normalized distribution yield is 8.4% (~$2.16/unit per year); t...

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Producing royalty and bonus stream
components.assumption_summary: Present value of after-trust royalty distributions under explicit volume, pellet-price, bonus, and discount-rate cases.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: Finite reserve and counterparty concentration reserve
components.assumption_summary: Deduction for depletion, operator concentration, interruption, and terminal-value uncertainty.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: Arbitration and bonus-payment option
components.assumption_summary: Base assigns no value to the undisclosed arbitration. Only a separately evidenced incremental recovery beyond ordinary royalties may enter the high sensitivity; ordinary base and bonus royalties remain in the producing stream.
components.cross_check: Do not promote the high case into base until a primary legal record states a claim, remedy, timing, and collectibility. The 2024 collected award is a closed, non-recurring dispute and is not a comparable current payoff.

components.label: Trust cash and other net claims
components.assumption_summary: Source-locked net trust reserve divided by source-locked units; refresh after every filing.
components.cross_check: Latest disclosed unallocated reserve divided by disclosed units reproduces the component exactly; no unsupported range is added.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Mesabi Trust 2026 Form 10-K and Annual Report, including the audited unallocated reserve bridge.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of MSB, including every identified operating claim, asset, liability, and option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Mesabi Trust 2026 Form 10-K: 13,120,010 units outstanding at April 21, 2026.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are included as separate components or within the stated equity basis.

valuation_proof.evidence: Primary Q1 2025/Q1 2026 royalty reports establish shipped tons, base/bonus outcomes, and the 2026 $71.70/ton bonus threshold; reserve life and full contractual base-tier table remain operator-controlled. Provisional $30/$35/$42 pending reproducible PV bridge. See MSB/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high values incorporate ownership, tax, funding, cycle, and realization adjustments described in the component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key producing_royalty_stream; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence or through-cycle results support economics below the low case or reveal an omitted claim.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Finite reserve and counterparty concentration reserve
valuation_proof.evidence: Finite claim and Cliffs/Northshore concentration confirmed in 10-K/8-K; no primary recoverable-tonnage or mine-life schedule. Provisional -$5/-$3/-$1 remains evidence-blocked. See evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high values incorporate ownership, tax, funding, cycle, and realization adjustments described in the component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key depletion_and_concentration_reserve; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence or through-cycle results support economics below the low case or reveal an omitted claim.

valuation_proof.evidence: The prior AAA award was paid in full on 2024-10-04: $71.185 million, or approximately $5.43 per unit (MSB 8-K filed 2024-10-17). The September 2025 arbitration is disclosed in MSB's 2025-09-26 8-K and 2026 Form 10-K Item 3, but neither filing states a claim amount, schedule, probability, or collectibility. Base value is therefore explicitly zero; the $13 high case is an unapproved upside sensitivity only. See MSB/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high values incorporate ownership, tax, funding, cycle, and realization adjustments described in the component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key arbitration_and_bonus_option; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Dismissal, an adverse award, a settlement below the separately modeled incremental amount, or a remedy limited to royalties already accrued in the producing stream.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Scenario range includes delay and failure outcomes.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Starter probability is an explicit analyst judgment pending base-rate and milestone reconciliation.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining capital and financing are deducted in the stated option range.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Trust cash and other net claims
valuation_proof.evidence: The 2026 Annual Report reports a $20.403 million unallocated reserve, including $19.751 million unallocated cash, across 13,120,010 units, or approximately $1.555 per unit.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high values incorporate ownership, tax, funding, cycle, and realization adjustments described in the component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key trust_cash_and_other_claims; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Material contingent legal costs or other senior liabilities consume the unallocated reserve.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Steel mills through-cycle value
components.assumption_summary: Normalize shipments, spread, utilization, maintenance capital, and tax across a full steel cycle rather than capitalizing 2025 or spot margins.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: Downstream steel products
components.assumption_summary: Value downstream earnings separately using normalized backlog, margins, and maintenance capital.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: Raw materials and recycling
components.assumption_summary: Use through-cycle DRI, scrap, and brokerage economics after sustaining capital.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: Net debt and lease claims
components.assumption_summary: Deduct all debt and finance leases net of cash and short-term investments; range reflects required operating liquidity.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: New-project ramp and replacement-cost optionality
components.assumption_summary: Credit only probability-weighted after-tax cash returns above the cost of capital, net of remaining spend and ramp losses.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

components.label: Supply-response and downcycle reserve

deep_dive.executive_summary:
OR Royalties is a high-margin precious metals royalty company with a producing base near 81,000 GEOs and a filing-guided path toward 120,000 to 135,000 GEOs by 2030. FY2025 operating cash flow was $1.31 per share; dividends yield about 6% at today's price. Commodity prices and operator execution remain the main swing factors.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Long Island cemetery (501c13) with ~$162M FY2024 net assets; OTC $575 quote; IRR pending verified share count and Form 990 PDFs.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Pinelawn is an operating cemetery with Form 990 economics of roughly $43 million revenue and $162 million net assets in FY2024. The OTC quote is about $575. We cannot publish a base annual return until shares outstanding and the cash dividend are verified: aggregator figures near 3.46 million shares imply a ~$2 billion equity value and a cash dividend bill that the Form 990 income cannot support. IRR is pending. Dhando is partial (real operations and assets; identity and per-share claims unclear). Stance: watch, with a Vicki brief to pull Form 990 PDFs.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Form 990 net assets (accounting floor)
components.assumption_summary: 990 net assets as reference floor for the nonprofit entity, not proven liquidation NAV.
components.cross_check: Range must reconcile to primary filings and realization path before IC approval.

components.label: Cemetery operating franchise
components.assumption_summary: Modest capitalization of cemetery cash earnings; land value is separate.
components.cross_check: Range must reconcile to primary filings and realization path before IC approval.

components.label: Land fair-value gap above 990 carrying amounts
components.assumption_summary: Option on land mark-up; not double-counted inside form990_net_assets.
components.cross_check: Range must reconcile to primary filings and realization path before IC approval.

components.label: OTC illiquidity and nonprofit-governance reserve
components.assumption_summary: Large reserve until share count and dividend cash facts are verified.
components.cross_check: Range must reconcile to primary filings and realization path before IC approval.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Form 990 FY2024 extract via ProPublica (PDF pending). Not SEC GAAP.

economic_claim.description: One provisional Pinelawn Cemetery equity unit using aggregator share count until the true economic unit count is verified.
economic_claim.unit_source: Aggregator ~3.46M shares — CONFLICTS with dividend cash math versus FY2024 NI $5.3M [HUMAN REVIEW — do not size on this denominator]
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Nonprofit cemetery: 990 net assets + operations + land gap less illiquidity/governance reserve. Market price $575 is not reconciled until share count is fixed.

valuation_proof.evidence: ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer EIN 11-1190044 FY2024 net assets $161,903,602 / provisional 3.46M shares ≈ $46.8. PDF not yet local [HUMAN REVIEW].
valuation_proof.adjustment: Accounting reference only until PDF and share count verified.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key form990_net_assets; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Verified share count or Schedule D shows net assets per economic unit far from this band.

valuation_proof.evidence: FY2024 revenue ~$42.9M, expenses ~$37.5M, NI ~$5.3M. Capitalization of through-cycle owner cash pending audited statements.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Dividend claim must reconcile to NI.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key cemetery_operations; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Operations cannot sustain distributions anywhere near the indicated $45.40 dividend under the provisional share count.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Land fair-value gap above 990 carrying amounts
valuation_proof.evidence: Long Island cemetery land often carried below market on Form 990; Schedule D marks required. Wide option range until appraisal evidence.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Wide until Schedule D / appraisal.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key land_market_to_990_gap; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Schedule D / appraisal shows no material gap above 990 carrying value.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Realization only via control transaction or gradual plot sales; not a dated payoff.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Base assigns modest probability of a material mark-up; high case is appraisal upside.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No owner-funded development capital modeled; cemetery ops fund maintenance.

valuation_proof.evidence: OTC Pink, tiny volume, 501(c)(13) constraints on distributions/control, unresolved share count that makes a $45.40 indicated dividend mathematically implausible at 3.46M shares versus $5.3M NI.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Dominates until facts clear.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key illiquidity_and_governance_reserve; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Verified float and governance show minority common can realize NAV without the haircut.

Marvin base: 15.4% per year at $13.79 on $1.65/share filings-based owner cash, 9 times exit, 2.5% / 2% growth. Segment sum-of-parts (status quo): ~$49/share at peer-discounted multiples. Post-POC sale scenario (~$1.5B proceeds, base multiples): ~$83/share (or ~$52/share if core re-rates to only 6×). McIntyre Partnerships normalization (~$4/share by 2028) is context only, not our primary path. Moat unproven; dhando partial. Proposed...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Star Holdings trades ~54% below Q1 2026 book (~$19.88/sh) as a legacy iStar wind-down with a large Safehold stake; base case 10.4% per year if ~75% of book is returned over five years — watch pending related-party review.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Star Holdings is a legacy asset monetization vehicle with a marked Safehold stake, shrinking legacy portfolio, and active repurchases. Book value per share is about $19.88 at March 31, 2026; the quote near $9.15 is roughly 54% below book with a margin loan secured by the Safe shares but no operating moat. This is not a compounder: ten-year free-cash-flow math is the wrong primary lens. Our base case uses a five-year capital-return path to realize roughly 75% of book (~$15 per share) through asset sales, Safe dividends, and buybacks, implying about 10.4% per year from today's price. Downside...

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Safehold Inc. equity stake (~13.525M shares)
components.assumption_summary: Low/base/high vary SAFE mark and sale friction. High case is a rate-driven re-rating toward the March 2023 ~$25 deal mark, not a contractual put.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings and mark-to-market updates before IC approval.

components.label: MD&A monetizing loans, AFS securities, land, other properties
components.assumption_summary: Low/base/high vary recovery vs carrying on the MD&A schedule only; Magnolia/Asbury residual is separate.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings and mark-to-market updates before IC approval.

components.label: Magnolia Green and residual Asbury land / operating real estate
components.assumption_summary: Base applies a milder haircut than carrying because lot sales clear above COS; high adds partial depreciation / entitlement uplift; low remains distressed.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings and mark-to-market updates before IC approval.

components.label: Cash and restricted cash
components.assumption_summary: Cash is near carrying; low allows modest restricted-cash leakage.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings and mark-to-market updates before IC approval.

components.label: Safe Credit Facility and Margin Loan Facility
components.assumption_summary: Low allows PIK accretion / incremental draws; high assumes modest paydown from sales.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings and mark-to-market updates before IC approval.

components.label: External management fees, termination overhang, and sale friction
components.assumption_summary: Base assumes Safehold remains manager through most of the wind-down; high assumes post-2027-03-31 competitive management / lower fee drag; low keeps termination overhang + high friction.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings and mark-to-market updates before IC approval.

components.label: Zero-carry claims and entitlement surplus option
components.assumption_summary: Failure value zero; high credits modest surplus/zero-carry recovery.
components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings and mark-to-market updates before IC approval.

economic_value.accounting_reference: STHO Q1 2026 Form 10-Q; FY2025 Form 10-K; 8-K 2026-04-01 Asbury deconsolidation.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of STHO, including every identified asset, liability, and option in the wind-down vehicle.
economic_claim.unit_source: STHO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260508_rpt20260331_acc0001953366_26_000010.htm (May 6, 2026)
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: SAFE marked gross; Margin Loan and Safe Credit in senior_debt; monetizing vs Magnolia/Asbury residual split to avoid double count.

valuation_proof.evidence: FACT spin/merger mark: SAFE closed $25.80 on 2023-03-31 (Yahoo; post 0.16 split on merger day), ~$25/share round number. At 13,525,499 implied shares that is $338.1M = $27.99/STHO share vs current Yahoo SAFE $16.93 → $18.95/STHO share (about -32% below deal mark). FACT Q1 10-Q Note 7: ~13.5M shares / 18.8%, FV $183.0M at $13.53 on 2026-03-31. INFERENCE: SAFE owns long-duration ground leases (typically ~99-year; IR decks cite ~91–92 year weighted-average extended lease term), so the equity mark is rate-sensitive — lower discount rates support re-rating toward/above the deal mark. JUDGMENT cases: low ~$12 SAFE forced sale; base near current mark with friction; high ~$25 deal-mark path. Margin Loan $92.8M first-priority pledge (Note 9).
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key safehold_equity_stake.
valuation_proof.falsifier: SAFE fails to re-rate with lower rates, or forced sale/collateral path nets below the low case.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: MD&A monetizing loans, AFS securities, land, other properties
valuation_proof.evidence: FACT Q1 MD&A monetizing portfolio: loan $16.1M + ten AFS debt securities $38.2M + land $14.4M + two properties $2.7M = $71.4M ($5.91/sh). Zero-carry loans/equity interests disclosed with no assurance of recovery. Q1 land-development sales revenue $11.0M / COS $9.3M; Surfhouse mezz $10.6M repaid 3/27/2026 (8-K). Source: 10-Q MD&A; Note 5/6; 8-K 2026-04-01. INFERENCE from 10-K/10-Q land-sale margins: the $14.4M monetizing land sleeve may realize above carrying; loans/AFS remain nearer mark-to-market/par.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key legacy_monetizing_portfolio.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Sale/repayment proceeds imply recovery below $3.50/sh on this bucket.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Realization is modeled through the five-year wind-down horizon, with specific sale and repayment timing remaining uncertain.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The recovery range applies haircuts to the disclosed loans, AFS securities, land, and properties; the base case relies on repayment and land-sale evidence while zero-carry claims receive no value here.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No incremental development capital is attributed to this monetizing bucket; sale costs and wind-down friction are deducted separately in wind_down_fee_and_friction_reserve.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Magnolia Green and residual Asbury land / operating real estate
valuation_proof.evidence: FACT YE2025 10-K Note 5 dispositions: land development revenue vs cost of sales 2025 $46.4M / $28.8M (+$17.6M), 2024 $60.0M / $48.7M (+$11.3M), 2023 $72.4M / $62.7M (+$9.7M). FACT Q1 2026: land development revenue $11.0M vs COS $9.3M (+$1.7M). INFERENCE: actively selling land/lots above carrying cost of sales — GAAP book understates realizable value for the monetizing land/development sleeve. FACT Q1 accum. depreciation: RE $28.784M + L&D $10.547M = $39.3M (~$3.26/sh) — upper bound on pure depreciation add-back for depreciable assets; land itself is not depreciated the same way. FACT 10-K: Company did not sell operating real estate (buildings) in 2025 or 2023 — Asbury hotel/ops premium to book is unproven by closed sales. FACT residual carrying after removing MD&A monetizing land/other ≈ $12.72/sh. Sources: 10-K Note 5; 10-Q Notes 4–5; Item 1 Magnolia/Asbury.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key magnolia_asbury_development_ops.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Monetization clears below $4/sh after costs on this residual bucket.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Land and operating-real-estate realization is modeled over the five-year wind-down horizon; no immediate sale of the remaining operating real estate is assumed.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The base case gives partial credit to carrying value because 2023 through Q1 2026 land and lot sales exceeded cost of sales, while the low case allows slow or distressed realization and the high case approaches carrying.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: The component is valued after its carrying-value haircut; no separate development budget is added, and fee and sale-friction deductions remain in wind_down_fee_and_friction_reserve.

valuation_proof.evidence: FACT Q1 balance sheet / Note 8: cash $46.378M + restricted cash $15.681M = $62.059M ($5.14/sh). Prior research incorrectly used YE $67.2M for Q1.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key cash_and_restricted.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Restricted cash is unavailable for equity or required as additional margin collateral.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Safe Credit Facility and Margin Loan Facility
valuation_proof.evidence: FACT Note 9: Safe Credit Facility $115M at 8.00% (10% if incremental drawn), maturity March 2028; Margin Loan $92.8M at SOFR+3.50%, PIK option (+25bp after PIK), secured by first-priority pledge of Safe Shares, maturity March 2028, delayed-draw capacity ~$15.8M. Total debt obligations net $207.0M ($17.13/sh). CORRECTED: prior -1.19/sh was wrong; (cash-debt)/shares ≈ -12.0. Exact LTV/collateral-posting triggers not numerically disclosed (only that Mar-2025 amendment eased them).
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key senior_debt.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Debt claims exceed $17.50/sh or collateral call forces SAFE sale below low stake value.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: External management fees, termination overhang, and sale friction
valuation_proof.evidence: FACT Note 1: annual auto-renewal each March 31; terminate without cause with ≥180 days notice and ≥2/3 independent trustee vote. Before 2027-03-31, termination fee = $55M minus fees paid (≈$5M overhang after $50M paid through 2026-03-31). After 2027-03-31, that termination-fee trigger no longer applies to without-cause terminations under the disclosed formula. FACT current term fee $7.5M; thereafter 2% GBV ex-SAFE. JUDGMENT: H2 2026 is the practical window to run a manager RFP so a replacement can be effective at/after the 2027-03-31 fee cliff — not an immediate free option today. Safe Governance Agreement still restricts certain SAFE-share transfers. Cash taxes de minimis (DTA allowance to zero; $0.2M paid in 2025).
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key wind_down_fee_and_friction_reserve.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Fees, termination payment, or tax leakage exceed $2.50/sh present value.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Zero-carry claims and entitlement surplus option
valuation_proof.evidence: FACT MD&A: group of loans/equity interests recorded at zero carrying value; no assurance of recovery. FACT Magnolia entitled 3,550 units / ~193 commercial acres — surplus above residual carrying is not contracted. Only incremental value beyond magnolia_asbury_development_ops carrying cases.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key zero_carry_and_entitlement_option.
valuation_proof.falsifier: No surplus to carrying and zero-carry assets remain worthless.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Any recovery is treated as a contingent result within the five-year wind-down horizon, not as current cash or a contracted asset sale.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The low case assigns zero, the base assigns only $0.20 per share, and the high case requires incremental recovery from zero-carry claims or entitlement surplus beyond Magnolia/Asbury carrying cases.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No incremental owner-funded development spend is credited to this option; any continuing holding, entitlement, management, or sale costs are captured in the existing operating and wind-down reserve components.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Swedish industrial serial acquirer of niche subsidiaries — decentralized Pabrai-style model with 2025–2026 margin/FCF turnaround underway; organic sales still negative while unprofitable units are pruned.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Teqnion is a proven decentralized serial acquirer with a strengthening margin story: FY2025 net sales +15% (nine acquisitions), EBITA +36%, organic -5% from deliberate exits, and equity ratio 42.8%. Goodwill impairment 73 MSEK (Q3 2025) flags past overpayment risk. At ~158 SEK, base expected annual return (Lawrence) is 18.14% on 10.08 SEK FY2025 FCF/share, below the 15% accumulate band. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable at the portfolio level; asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is partial with M&A risk. We propose watch unless price falls toward ~130 SEK for a ~15% base IRR.

The only reason this shell has any value at all is a large, protected tax loss carryforward that Steel Partners could someday use if it drops a profitable business into the company. tickers.one_line_thesis: Permian land, royalty, and water toll at ~$393: durable moat, falsifier-adjusted base -1.0%, NAV overlay ~$211/sh vs price — watch for better entry.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Producing oil and gas royalties
components.assumption_summary: Capital-free royalty cash flow. Base: 4% revenue growth in years 1-5, 2.5% in years 6-10, 78% after-tax owner-cash margin, 22x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: Do not add undeveloped royalty inventory that is already contributing to the $411.7M producing run-rate.

components.label: Existing surface, easement, and land-use operations
components.assumption_summary: Base: 6% growth in years 1-5 and 3.5% thereafter, 65% after-tax owner-cash conversion, 20x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: Named uncontracted corridors and data-center projects remain in separate option components.

components.label: Produced-water royalty stream
components.assumption_summary: Capital-free stream. Base: 12% revenue growth in years 1-5 and 5% thereafter, 78% after-tax owner-cash margin, 24x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: The base growth rate is far below the 2018-2025 historical volume CAGR and must be tested against basin oil volumes, water-oil ratios, AMI coverage, and realized royalty per barrel.

components.label: Cash and equity-method investment
components.cross_check: No debt is disclosed in the FY2025 balance-sheet bridge.

components.label: Operated water sales and services
components.assumption_summary: Base: 10% revenue growth in years 1-5 and 4% thereafter, 35% after-tax owner-cash margin after maintenance capital, 30% incremental after-tax ROIC, 18x terminal owner cash, 10% discount rate.
components.cross_check: The model charges growth investment explicitly; higher growth creates value only when the assumed incremental after-tax return on capital is earned.

components.label: Contracted data-center land-financing receivable
components.cross_check: Uncontracted data-center and power development remains in the separate project option.

components.label: Visible undeveloped royalty inventory
components.assumption_summary: 45% of the comparable-NAV residual after subtracting producing royalty value; the 70,000-NRA label remains provisional until a tract-level inventory roll-forward is disclosed.
components.cross_check: Producing royalties plus visible and dormant inventory reconcile to the total comparable value of all 224,000 NRA, so the same royalty acre cannot be capitalized twice.

components.label: Dormant and long-dated royalty inventory
components.assumption_summary: 55% of the comparable-NAV residual; duration and tract-quality uncertainty are expressed through the portfolio unit marks rather than an arbitrary zero value.
components.cross_check: Producing royalties plus visible and dormant inventory equal approximately $81/$130/$243 per share, corresponding to $25k/$40k/$75k per total NRA.

components.label: Residual uncontracted surface land
components.assumption_summary: 882,000 acres at $1,500/$3,000/$5,718 per acre less 20%/15%/10% portfolio realization friction.
components.cross_check: Per-acre marks represent only residual value after recurring operations and named project options; they are not a second capitalization of existing easement cash flow.

components.label: Future infrastructure corridors and easements
components.assumption_summary: Probability-weighted successful-project PV less remaining development cost.
components.cross_check: Each project graduates into existing surface/easement operations once contracted.

components.label: Data-center, power, and water co-location option
components.assumption_summary: Success value is probability-weighted for customer, power, interconnection, water, capital, permitting, and commissioning milestones.
components.cross_check: The separately modeled receivable and the balance-sheet value of the Bolt investment are excluded from this incremental option.

components.label: Desalination and water-technology platform
components.assumption_summary: Probability-weighted platform value less remaining commercialization cost; the asset state is demonstration, not research-only.
components.cross_check: Commercial value requires repeatable unit economics and a capital-light license, cost-plus, JV, or spin structure; technical success alone is insufficient.

components.label: Corporate, tax, and realization reserve
components.cross_check: Low case absorbs more of the gross option value as realization cost.

components.label: Water infrastructure replacement-cost cross-check
components.cross_check: The operated-water DCF already capitalizes returns on installed infrastructure.

economic_value.accounting_reference: TPL's 2025 10-K reports approximately $840M net acquired royalty interests, while the legacy 1/16 and 1/128 perpetual interests remain at zero carrying value.

economic_claim.description: One post-December-2025-split common share has the same economic claim as every other common share. Q1 2026 diluted shares were approximately 69.0 million; TPL had net cash and an undrawn credit facility.
economic_claim.unit_source: valuation.inputs.shares_outstanding
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: The complete additive component schedule includes operating assets, financial assets, debt or financing claims, minority interests where applicable, and realization reserves before division by the stated economic units.

valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 oil and gas royalty revenue was $411.7M. TPL bears no drilling capital; the scenario driver is royalty revenue growth, after-tax owner-cash conversion, duration, and discount rate.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The producing DCF is subtracted from total royalty NAV before visible and dormant residual values are added.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Surface land, existing uses, and future corridors

valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Existing contracted uses, residual land, and uncontracted corridors are separate economic claims.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Tesla is a dual-segment electric vehicle and energy storage platform with FY2025 free cash flow near $1.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Tesla is a dual-segment electric vehicle and energy storage platform with FY2025 free cash flow near $1.76 per share but declining net income versus 2024. We normalize starting owner cash to $1.75 per share and exclude robotaxi or Full Self-Driving terminal value from the Lawrence base path. At ~$350 per share, we expect -21.19% per year over seven years on the base case, far below our accumulate bar. Segment overlay shows energy growing faster than auto; FSD is probability-weighted at 12% in the option layer only. Moat eroding. Cycle mid. Dhando none at spot. Stance watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary: WaterBridge is Delaware Basin produced-water infrastructure with strong post-combination operating proof: Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA $102.9 million on 2,460 MBbl/d volumes. FY2025 adjusted EBITDA was $254 million, but GAAP losses, $1.47 billion debt, and Five Point sponsor control complicate economic attribution for Class A holders. At ~$30 (NYSE, June 2, 2026), we expect 13.92% per year on maintenance-adjusted FCF per share of $2.13 (6%/4% growth, 14x exit). Cap-rate cross-check: ~9% EBITDA yield on ~$2.9B EV looks fairly valued for merchant midstream. Moat is unproven and dhando pending. We ...

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Existing produced-water network enterprise value
components.cross_check: Q1 2026 EBITDA was $102.9M on 2.5M bbl/d; merchant infrastructure is not a regulated rate base.

components.label: Net debt
components.cross_check: Base net debt is approximately $1.424B, or $11.54 per economic unit.

components.label: Speedway, Devon, and contracted growth beyond 2026
components.cross_check: Only value beyond the normalized 2026 EBITDA run-rate is added; the core network value already includes the 2026 guide.

components.label: Growth-capex execution reserve
components.cross_check: Released only after cohort-level capital and EBITDA are disclosed.

components.label: Network replacement-cost cross-check
components.cross_check: Not additive to enterprise value.

economic_value.accounting_reference: The network includes approximately 2,500 miles of pipeline, 197 facilities, more than 4.5M bpd of capacity, and long-term dedications; tangible book value is not the primary valuation method.

economic_claim.description: Each Class A share corresponds economically to one OpCo unit. Valuation uses 47.016 million Class A plus 76.440 million Class B/OpCo units. The prior Class-A-only denominator materially overstated consolidated free cash flow per listed share.
economic_claim.unit_source: valuation.inputs.shares_outstanding
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: The complete additive component schedule includes operating assets, financial assets, debt or financing claims, minority interests where applicable, and realization reserves before division by the stated economic units.

valuation_proof.evidence: 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance is $425M-$465M. Values use $380M at 8x, $445M at 10x, and $500M at 12x.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The $10/$18/$28 per-share replacement-cost cross-check is embedded and never added to enterprise value.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Utilization, incremental after-tax return on capital, or contract economics fall below the low-case assumptions.

valuation_proof.evidence: March 2026 principal debt was $1.475B and cash was $50.7M.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Deducted once from network enterprise value.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The obligation, financing claim, tax, or realization friction is materially larger than the low-case reserve.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Speedway, Devon, and contracted projects beyond 2026
valuation_proof.evidence: 2026 capex guidance is $430M-$490M and includes Speedway plus projects supporting a Devon agreement beginning in 2027.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: 2026 guidance and projects already contributing to it stay in the core network.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Discounting, realization friction, and the component assumptions reflect the time required to contract, develop, or monetize the claim.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The component low/base/high range or milestone driver explicitly risks realization; uncertainty widens the range rather than creating a zero.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining owner-funded capital is deducted in the driver where disclosed; otherwise the range and realization reserve reflect the unresolved capital burden.

valuation_proof.evidence: Reserve for cost overruns, delayed in-service dates, and returns below the cost of capital on the $430M-$490M program.

valuation_proof.overlap_control: Offsets only project execution risk, not recurring maintenance capital already reflected in normalized owner cash.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The obligation, financing claim, tax, or realization friction is materially larger than the low-case reserve.

valuation_proof.evidence: Pipelines, disposal capacity, rights-of-way, and permits provide an embedded asset floor but are already monetized by the core network.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
At $581.87, we expect -20.06% per year over seven years on fiscal 2025 owner cash. Stance: watch.

Western Digital is a pure-play HDD duopoly after the Sandisk spin, with fiscal 2025 continuing revenue $9.52 billion (+50.6%) and owner cash $3.56 per share. Nine-month fiscal 2026 revenue $9.17 billion (+32.8%) and $1.92 billion of buybacks show the cycle is firm, but the moat is only stable and dhando partial at this price. The 28.8% Sandisk stake is an option outside base cash flow.

tickers.company: The Central and Eastern Europe Fund, Inc.
tickers.one_line_thesis: Shadow-NAV CEF: price tracks reported NAV with Russia at zero, but a Moscow mark-to-market illustration implies roughly a mid-20s percent discount to economic value.

deep_dive.executive_summary: CEE is a shadow-net-asset-value idea disguised as a near-par closed-end fund. At about $20.48 versus reported net asset value of about $21.32, the stock looks only a few percent cheap. That comparison ignores Russia. Using the last detailed schedule (July 2025) and current Moscow exchange prints, a mark-to-market illustration adds roughly $5 to $9 per share of Russian equity value before any friction. That implies an economic net asset value near the mid-$20s to about $30, or a mid-20s to low-30s percent discount to price. The base case in this report still values Russia at zero until a hum...

items.next_gap_question: Reconcile trust units, contractual royalty tiers, reserves, production, realized pellet prices, bonuses, taxes, and depletion.
items.next_gap_progress_note: partially_met: 2026 Form 10-K/Exhibit 13 reconciles 13,120,010 units and a $20.403M unallocated reserve; the 2025-05-02 and 2026-05-04 royalty-report exhibits reconcile Q1 shipped tons, base royalty, bonus royalty, and the $71.70/ton 2026 bonus threshold (MSB/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.md). The primary record still lacks Cliffs/Northshore's recoverable-reserve, production-cadence, realized-pellet-price, and full royalty-tier schedules needed to reproduce the $30/$35/$42 producing-stream and -$5/-$3/-$1 depletion ranges.

items.next_gap_question: What net economic claim belongs to AZLCZ after project ownership, partner waterfalls, taxes, and related-party claims?
items.next_gap_progress_note: partially_met: FY2025 audited income-tax-basis statements reconcile 141,666 shares, $3.657M rentals, $2.343M excess cash-basis revenue over expenses, $6.183M related-party notes receivable, investments in Apache Railroad/Aztec Minerals, and renewable lease stages (AZLCZ/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.json). They do not disclose project-by-project ownership, partner waterfall, capital obligation, tax, and cash-remittance bridges from gross project economics to one AZLCZ share.

items.next_gap_question: What enforceable economics accrue to LB from the Alpha Digital campus?
items.next_gap_progress_note: partially_met: FY2025 10-K establishes that prospective digital projects are expected to use surface-use agreements and that LB does not expect significant project capital; the local primary packet does not contain an executed Alpha Digital agreement with rent/participation, MW phasing, commencement, capital, or termination economics (LB/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.json). The component base is now explicitly zero; Samsung options remain capacity options, not operating rent.

items.next_gap_question: How much royalty acreage is economically developable, at what royalty interest, and on what cadence?
items.next_gap_progress_note: partially_met: FY2025 10-K confirms approximately 224,000 total NRA, comprising 1/128 NPRI under approximately 85,000 acres, 1/16 NPRI under approximately 371,000 acres, and approximately 33,000 additional 1/8-normalized NRA (TPL/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.json). The filing still lacks a tract/county roll-forward allocating each claim to producing, visible undeveloped, or dormant acreage with activity, probability, and timing.

items.next_gap_question: What cash return has each major project cohort earned on total installed capital?
items.next_gap_progress_note: partially_met: WBI's 2025 Form 10-K reconciles $254.0M adjusted EBITDA, $159.7M operating cash flow, $218.6M investing cash outflow, 1.622M bbl/day handling volume, and a 2,651-mile / 201-facility asset base (WBI/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.md). It does not disclose project-level invested capital, in-service date, utilization, EBITDA, maintenance capital, taxes, or contract contribution, so cohort ROIC cannot be reproduced.

advantaged_banks_screener.criteria: Advantaged / competitive-franchise banks (seed list). Metrics from SEC companyfacts: deposits, noninterest deposits (DDA share), approx. cost of deposits (annual InterestExpenseDeposits ÷ period-end Deposits), tangible book, ROE, P/TBV; market cap = SEC shares × Yahoo chart price. Low-cost flag: cost of deposits < 1.5% or DDA share > 30%. Framed by Moore community-bank handbook (_system/reference/investment-wisdom/moore/). Seed: _system/reference/market-data/screens/advantaged_banks_seed.csv.
